sentences.base_language: This weekend, do you want to go to the beach?
sentences.target_language: Este fin de semana, ¿quieres ir a la playa?
sentences.grammarNote: Demonstrative adjectives agree with the noun's gender: este/esta (this), ese/esa (that). 'Este fin de semana' (this weekend — masculine), 'esta noche' (tonight — feminine), 'este año' (this year — masculine).

sentences.base_language: I can't. I have to work on Saturday.
sentences.target_language: No puedo. Tengo que trabajar el sábado.
sentences.grammarNote: 'Tener que + infinitive' = to have to do something. It expresses obligation or necessity: 'tengo que trabajar' (I have to work), 'tienes que estudiar' (you have to study). It's one of the most common ways to express obligation in Spanish.

sentences.base_language: We can go in the morning.
sentences.grammarNote: 'Poder + infinitive' = can / to be able to. Poder is o→ue: puedo, puedes, puede, podemos, podéis, pueden. 'Podemos ir' = We can go.

sentences.base_language: I prefer the afternoon. It's less crowded.
sentences.target_language: Prefiero la tarde. Hay menos gente.

sentences.grammarNote: 'Trae' is the informal tú imperative of traer (to bring). Common informal (tú) imperatives: trae (bring), come (eat), bebe (drink), habla (speak), escucha (listen), espera (wait).
